issue_title: Admin Imports Need Updating for Django 1.8
issue_body:
There have been some changes in Django 1.8 relating to the `django.contrib.gis` library, which of course includes the `GeometryField` found in `django.contrib.gis.db.models` module.

I am using django-leaflet in a project that does not make use of GeoDjango (`django.contrib.gis`) and so we do not have GEOS, PostGIS, PROJ.4 and other required libraries installed. This hasn't prevented us from using django-leaflet until upgrading from Django 1.7.4 to 1.8.0.

The problem comes from django-leaflet's `admin.py` file:
```python
try:
    from django.contrib.gis.db.models import GeometryField
except ImportError:
    GeometryField = type(object)
```

In Django 1.8.0, this import statement raises an `ImproperlyConfigured` exception instead of an `ImportError` when GeoDjango isn't installed.

Unless I'm missing something here, this seems like a straightforward fix:
```python
try:
    from django.contrib.gis.db.models import GeometryField
except (ImportError, ImproperlyConfigured):
    GeometryField = type(object)
```

issue_title: Store instance of "geometry_field_class" in a variable
issue_body:
I am trying to set a new layer with some javascript code. So I took the advise from the README
> If you need a reusable customization of widgets maps, first override the JavaScript field behaviour by
> extending L.GeometryField, then in Django subclass the LeafletWidget to specify the custom 
> geometry_field_class.

In my customization I have a function ```addExternalLayer(layer)```. And now I would like to be able to call this function from somewhere in my javascript code.

So, I think I would need the instance of the my extended L.GeometryField. But as far as I can see in your code, you are not storing this instance in some variable which I can use later.
https://github.com/makinacorpus/django-leaflet/blob/master/leaflet/templates/leaflet/widget.html#L20
```
(new {{ geometry_field_class}}({{ module }})).addTo(map);
```
So I changed this line to
```
geometry_field_instance = new {{ geometry_field_class }}({{ module }});
geometry_field_instance.addTo(map);
```
and now I'm able to call ```geometry_field_instance.addExternalLayer(L.marker([lat,lng]))``` and this removes all existing layers and adds my external layer as new one.

So, should I prepare a pull request with this change or do you have a better solution for my problem? 
I'm using a PointField and I would like provide the LatLng by myself and add a new Point.

issue_title: pipenv shell failing due to SyntaxError: invalid syntax
issue_body:
When trying to run pipenv shell it fail to do so with error message below:
pipenv shell --verbose
  File "<string>", line 1
    c:\python27\python.exe -c "from pew.pew import prevent_path_errors; prevent_path_errors()"
     ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax

##### Environment

1. Windows 7
2. Python 2.7.14
3. pipenv, version 11.1.4

##### Steps to replicate

just try to run pipenv shell.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I'm pretty much disappointed. This project used to be a very good one but when a package manager doesn't understand how important semver is I don't know if I can trust this package manager knowing anything else about packaging anymore.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Please go back to semantic versioning or at least explain why the hell you think date based versions are a good idea.

Advantages: 

1. `--save`, `--save-dev`, `--save-exact` could be implemented without much effort

2. common file format, so we could use existing editor capabilities

3. In the future we possible could converge existing `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py` and this file, into one. 

This is a little specs, I've created, maybe it will be of any use (it's not complete, VCS has to be expanded, maybe I've forgotten some other cases)
```toml

[dependencies]
# plain, command: pip install --save Django
django = ">=1.10.1"

# command: pip install --save-exact Django 
django = "==1.10.1"

# command: pip install --save Django==1.10.1 
# Any specified specifier goes exactly (even without --save-exact)
django2 = "==1.10.1"

# full version
SomeProject = {version = "==5.4"} # denotes what version exists as a part of package metadata

# environment markers
SomeProject2 = {version = "==5.4", markers = {python_version = "< 2.7", sys_platform = "win32"}}
# OR
[dependencies.SomeProject2.markers]
sys_version = "< 2.7"
sys_platform = "win32"

# particular file
# all non-obvious dependencies should be specified as a table (not inline), it's more verbose, which is a good thing here
[dependencies.numpy] # appears after --save
file = "./downloads/numpy-1.9.2-cp34-none-win32.whl"

# OR 

numpy = {file = "./downloads/numpy-1.9.2-cp34-none-win32.whl"}
wxpython = {file = "http://wxpython.org/Phoenix/snapshot-builds/wxPython_Phoenix-3.0.3.dev1820+49a8884-cp34-none-win_amd64.whl"}

# per-requirement overrides
#FooProject >= 1.2 --global-option="--no-user-cfg" \
#                  --install-option="--prefix='/usr/local'" \
#                  --install-option="--no-compile"
# maps to 
FooProject = {version = ">=1.2", setup_py_options = """--global-option="--no-user-cfg"
                                                    --install-option="--prefix='/usr/local'"
                                                    --install-option="--no-compile"""} # multiline string, only multiline that in valid in TOML

# OR
# if used in conjunction with --save (it's automatically generated, that's why doesn't really matter if it's verbose or not)
[dependencies.FooProject] 
version = ">=1.2"
setup_py_options = """--global-option="--no-user-cfg"
                    --install-option="--prefix='/usr/local'"
                    --install-option="--no-compile"""

# if necessary to add hashes
# https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/reference/pip_install/#hash-checking-mode
hashes = """ # maybe convert to array
--hash=sha256:2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824 \
--hash=sha256:486ea46224d1bb4fb680f34f7c9ad96a8f24ec88be73ea8e5a6c65260e9cb8a7
"""

# VCS 
flask = {git = "git repo", revision = "hash"}

# extras (dev, test, production), dev could be special-cased as [dev_dependencies]
[extras.PDF]
ReportLab = ">=1.2"


```

I recall a long discussion on #43 and elsewhere, where a decent consensus was reached to change to freeze files, so as to avoid confusion with [file locking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File_locking). I may be confused, but this seems to have been unilaterally reverted? I can't find any further discussion.

There is no benefit in not including the extension (a sense of mystery?).
In fact you will only have things to gain (editor highlighting, etc).

ref: Initially opened [here](https://github.com/kennethreitz/pipenv/issues/49).

The --manifest-only option to distutils' sdist (only generate a MANIFEST) is documented at https://docs.python.org/3.6/distutils/sourcedist.html#manifest-related-options, and can be trivially tested using the example at https://docs.python.org/3.6/distutils/examples.html#pure-python-distribution-by-module.
In that example, replacing the distutils import by a setuptools import results, when invoking `python setup.py --manifest-only`, in
```
error: option --manifest-only not recognized
```

I just want to share my opinion on the recent breaking changes. Please revert them. An utility to install packages cannot directly depend from other packages which in turn depends from it. To me is simple logic. The dependency chain must stop somewhere. `setuptools` dependencies must be vendored. This is the root cause of all problems.

issue_title: Disable nspkg.pth magic when PEP-420 is available
issue_body:
In pypa/pip#1924, we find that setuptools is generating the `-nspkg.pth` files that are bootstrapping the namespace packages, but these files are unnecessary when PEP-420 is present (Python 3.3+) and in fact interfere with pure PEP-420 namespace packages.

It seems the solution may be simple - bypass this behavior when PEP-420 is detected.

issue_title: pluggable password storage format for ldiftree back end
issue_body:
The base classes for LDAP DIT entries found in :file:`ldaptor/entry.py` are hard-coded to store password hashes for simple BINDs in salted SHA1 format.  The entry is prefixed with "{SSHA}".  This is similar to one of the schemes that OpenLDAP uses, but allowing alternate schemes may be a good idea.

If the entry has no prefix, it can indicate the password itself is stored in plain text.  This is useful to developing and debugging.  Different hash formats could have different prefixes.  For example {SHA} for unsalted SHA or maybe {PBKDF2} for Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2.  

There would need to be a default storage format that is configurable.  That would indicate the format used when the LDAP modify password extended request is used.

Also, it may be nice to allow configuration of the attribute in which the password/hash is stored.  OpenLDAP allows this, but defaults to `userPassword`.

16.0.1 used to allow comparing a DistinguishedName with a text... but the implementation was broken as it relies on simple string comparison... and had no test coverage

The 16.0.1 implementation converting the DistinguishedName to a text and then compare it, instead of converting the text to a DistinguishedName and then do the comparison.

See the end of https://www.ldap.com/ldap-dns-and-rdns

Two DistinguishedName can still be compared.  This is only about things like

```
dn.DistinguishedName('dc=example,dc=com') > 'dc = example, dc = dc`
```

issue_title: Support encoding emojis without encoding as surrogate pairs
issue_body: Currently, if an emoji is in a YAML string, and run through `safeDump`, it will be converted into escaped surrogate pairs (i.e. `thiskey: "😀"` is dumped as `thiskey: "\uD83D\uDE00"`). This is caused by [lib/js-yaml/dumper.js#L468](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/bd32506/lib/js-yaml/dumper.js#L468), as using JS `charCodeAt` returns surrogate pairs by default (see https://mathiasbynens.be/notes/javascript-unicode). Would you be able to at least make this configurable, so that we could choose whether to convert into surrogate pairs or just write the emojis directly?

issue_title: Consider separating CLI from main package.
issue_body: The five most popular dependants of `js-yaml`, [`eslint`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/eslint), [`istanbul`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/istanbul), [`svgo`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/svgo), [`grunt`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/grunt), and [`cosmiconfig`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/cosmiconfig) do not seem to use the CLI.  Would it be reasonable to move the CLI for `js-yaml` to another package, something like `js-yaml-cli`?  Excluding the `argparse` dependency would save ~140 KB.

issue_body:
- [x] Code example (attached inline)
- [x] Relevant images (attached inline)
- [x] Operating system and version : Ubuntu 16.04
- [x] Python version : 2.7.12
- [x] scikit-image version : 0.13.0

The issue is if I try to do imshow_collection on all the images from a list it shows some garbage values. 
```
%matplotlib
from skimage import io
io.use_plugin('matplotlib')
io.imshow_collection(io.imread_collection(file_list))
```
This outputs in the following image:

![screenshot from 2017-06-09 11-06-39](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10602903/26988318-ba9e0eaa-4d03-11e7-96b8-bdddabc49b6b.png)

```
I have also tried the following 
```
%matplotlib
from skimage import io
io.use_plugin('matplotlib')
io.imshow_collection(io.imread_collection(file_list[:6]))
```
Suprisingly this gives the correct result, but changing 6 to 7 again outputs a garbage image (I posted above). My suspicion is that there is an upper limit on the number of files that can be handled by `io.imshow_collection()`.

The interpolation functions within **skimage/_shared/interpolation.pxd** treat the ``mode='constant'`` case  differently than the `NI_GeometricTransform` function defined in **scipy/ndimage/src/ni_interpolation.c** that gets called by functions such as `scipy.ndimage.map_coordinates`.

Specifically, any coordinates outside the range of the image will be assigned a value exactly equal to `cval` in the ndimage implementation.  For the skimage case, however, the values are interpolated between cval and the edge pixels.  I am not sure this is necessarily a bug, but rather an inconsistency with scipy and I was not sure if this is intended behaviour or not.

It is fairly simple to get the scipy-like behaviour with slight modifications.  e.g. in `bilinear_interpolation`, the following can be placed before the bilinear interpolation:
```python
if (minr < 0) or (maxr >= rows) or (minc < 0) or (maxc >= cols):
    return cval
else:
    ...
```
If you agree that this is a bug, I can make a pull request with solutions similar to the above

issue_title: Nupic wants loose dev requirements and concrete deployment requirements
issue_body:
https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/1069 documents a new issue that interferes with nupic developers' need to use their own choice of nupic.bindings build. 

We're going to prototype an approach that places loose requirements in nupic's setup.py and concrete requirements in its requirements.txt. 

References

issue_title: RFC elasticsearch: allow specifying of unicode character folding
issue_body:
While BibIndex is automatically folding unicode characters using the suboptimal `utf8_general_ci` collation (and in parallel master-related future PR I am going to propose to allow to configure in a simple way the `utf8_unicode_520_ci` collation for certain indexes, it would be great to take this into account into the early elasticsearch integration into Invenio. In particular it should be easy (and we should have good defaults out of the box) to set the collation/character folding in elasticsearch so that indexes are properly configured.

See: http://www.elasticsearch.org/guide/en/elasticsearch/guide/current/unicode-normalization.html

Originally by *[adeiana (@Osso)](https://github.com/Osso)* on 2012-05-29

issue_body:
Both INSPIRE and Zenodo use the following code (https://github.com/zenodo/zenodo/blob/master/zenodo/celery.py#L42-L44) to allow Sentry logging from inside workers.

If the overlay uses environment variables for configuration, the following line will be executed https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-config/blob/master/invenio_config/env.py#L64 which will then make logging not work.

I have narrowed down the problem to that specific line and commenting it out makes things work, but I don't know what is the correct way of fixing that problem.

CC @lnielsen

`celery_config` is an already defined fixture in [`celery.contrib.pytest`](https://github.com/celery/celery/blob/3fe5a20ca655aa3aeed60205b39ede2a21845df0/celery/contrib/pytest.py#L113), which overwrites the one defined in Invenio.

issue_title: Messages with only HTML (and no plain text) content from Sendgrid are rejected
issue_body:
Hi there,

It's took me a bit of time to track down this problem with the package. In the `sendgrid.py` backend, text content is required, while HTML is optional:
`            text = _decode_POST_value(request, 'text')`
`            html = _decode_POST_value(request, 'html', default='')`

This means that any message with only HTML encoded text is rejected by the server. If `text` is changed to include a default, like this: 
`            text = _decode_POST_value(request, 'text', default='')`

Then any type of message can be received.

Not sure if it's easier for me to do a PR for this, or just make the quick change when you're doing other tests, but I'd be happy to. Not sure if this is an issue the other backends.

Cheers

issue_title: "500 - Internal Server Error" when trying to use dataset version command
issue_body:
Repro:

```bash
mkdir -p repro
cd repro
touch hello.csv
kaggle d init
sed -i 's/INSERT_[A-Z]\+_HERE/test123/' dataset-metadata.json
kaggle d create
sleep 10
# Work around https://github.com/Kaggle/kaggle-api/issues/77
kaggle d metadata colinmorris/test123

kaggle d version -m 'no changes' -p .
```

Output looks like...

```
Starting upload for file hello.csv
0.00B [00:00, ?B/s]
Upload successful: hello.csv (0B)
500 - Internal Server Error
```

I tried adding some content to `hello.csv` before the final step (in case this had to do with using an empty file, or trying to create a version with no changes), but the result was the same.

I was running fuzz testing on [my Brotli implementation](https://github.com/dsnet/compress/tree/master/brotli) and noticed that the following input differs when decompressed by my version compared to the canonical one here.

The input causing issues (in hex): e2e184886a563080e0391603e430f9

When decompressed by the `google/brotli` version: 
`worldwhitemediafirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirst...`
When decoding by my implementation: 
`worldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorl...`

According to section 4 of the RFC:
>   When a distance symbol 0 appears, the distance it represents (i.e.,
   the last distance in the sequence of distances) is not pushed to the
   ring buffer of last distances

However, this seems contrary to [what the decoder currently does](https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/ccabf811ffcef087d9f10cf417b05271c2067a82/dec/decode.c#L1706-L1710). Which does the following:
```c
if (s->distance_code >= 0) {
	--s->dist_rb_idx;
	s->distance_code = s->dist_rb[s->dist_rb_idx & 3];
	goto postReadDistance;  /* We already have the implicit distance */
}
```

The logic in the C version seems contrary to the stated prose in the RFC, which makes no mention that an implicit zero *reverse* rotates the distance ring-buffer.

issue_title: Add result precision processing and comparison among geocoders
issue_body:
I suppose this is cornerstone issue of using geocoding at all. Sooner or later every programmer will face that chosen geocoder results won't cover all processed addresses, so he or she has to use several geocoders simultaneously. And at this point it's need to somehow compare results of different geocoders to select the more precision one programmatically.

Geocoders return precision together with coordinates. The problem is every geocoder service has its own precision scale. 
I suggest to create the table for geocoders precisions in csv/sqlite/excel file. Columns are geocoder services, rows are precisions. The intersection of rows/columns contains universal numeric rank, which the lower the better. E.g. 0 - house precision, 100 - street, 200 - district, 300 - city etc

And add this rank value to geopy.location.Location class.

After upgrading to raven==5.2.0, I'm encountering the following error:

```python
File "/lib/python2.7/site-packages/haystack/utils/app_loading.py", line 22, in is_app_or_model
    raise ImproperlyConfigured("'%s' isn't recognized as an app (<app_label>) or model (<app_label>.<model_name>)." % label)
django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: 'raven.contrib.django' isn't recognized as an app (<app_label>) or model (<app_label>.<model_name>).
```

This is due to the label value in apps.py containing periods:

```python
class RavenConfig(AppConfig):
    name = 'raven.contrib.django'
    label = 'raven.contrib.django'
    verbose_name = 'Raven'
```

Haystack uses the following to determine whether something is an app or model (haystack/utils/app_loading.py):

```python
def is_app_or_model(label):
    label_bits = label.split('.')

    if len(label_bits) == 1:
        return APP
    elif len(label_bits) == 2:
        return MODEL
    else:
        raise ImproperlyConfigured("'%s' isn't recognized as an app (<app_label>) or model (<app_label>.<model_name>)." % label)
```

Because of the periods, len(label_bits) returns 3 causing the error to be raised.

If the periods are removed in the label, the error goes away:

```python
class RavenConfig(AppConfig):
    name = 'raven.contrib.django'
    label = 'raven_contrib_django'
    verbose_name = 'Raven'
```

issue_title: name for correlated noise in config
issue_body:
Hi @rmjarvis - Do I understand correctly that the only way to make correlated noise using config is with the `COSMOS` noise option?  And that the `file_name`, `cosmos_scale`, and `variance` can be reset to any other correlated noise you want?

If so, I wonder if we could perhaps change the name of this from `COSMOS` to something more general?  The name implies that you can only do COSMOS-like noise, but it seems like it's much more powerful than that.  Given those inputs it seems you could make essentially any arbitrary correlated noise field provided that you have a description of its correlation function.

issue_title: Name of cells is not correctly saved in fst module
issue_body:
Here is an example. This problem has a significant impact on the analysis, since we cannot track back the cells that are analyzed.

```
from neurom import ezy
from neurom import fst

n = ezy.load_neuron('./test_data/neurolucida/sample.asc')
n1 = fst.load_neuron('./test_data/neurolucida/sample.asc')

n.name
Out: 'sample'

n1.name
AttributeError: 'Neuron' object has no attribute 'name'
```

The same applies for neurons in a population. We should be able to identify which neuron is analyzed, even when we study a whole population of them.

Hello,

I have a three node Patroni cluster which works fine. I've installed Patroni from pip, and I'm using PostgreSQL 9.5 from PGDG. I'm on Centos 7.2.

I'm simulating a total network outage with iptables, with DROP all INPUT and OUTPUT (keeping connections from my management machine, on lo0, and from each machine to itself). I can still run psql and connect locally to the local network IP.

Patroni seems to get the right idea, and I can see each node gets upset and keeps cycling through the other servers looking for an etcd connection. 

On the master I can see this (it loops):

```
2016-09-20 09:06:58,447 INFO: Reconnection allowed, looking for another server.
2016-09-20 09:06:58,447 INFO: Selected new etcd server http://192.168.2.246:2379
2016-09-20 09:07:00,117 WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=0, connect=None, read=None, redirect=0)) after connection broken by 'ConnectTimeoutError(<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x1a20fd0>, u'Connection to 192.168.2.246 timed out. (connect timeout=1.66666666667)')': /v2/keys/service/postgresql/leader
2016-09-20 09:07:01,787 ERROR: Request to server http://192.168.2.246:2379 failed: MaxRetryError(u"HTTPConnectionPool(host=u'192.168.2.246', port=2379): Max retries exceeded with url: /v2/keys/service/postgresql/leader (Caused by ConnectTimeoutError(<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x1a20810>, u'Connection to 192.168.2.246 timed out. (connect timeout=1.66666666667)'))",)
2016-09-20 09:07:01,787 INFO: Reconnection allowed, looking for another server.
2016-09-20 09:07:01,787 ERROR: Machines cache is empty, no machines to try.
2016-09-20 09:07:01,787 ERROR: failed to update leader lock
2016-09-20 09:07:01,793 INFO: demoting self because i do not have the lock and i was a leader
2016-09-20 09:07:01,795 WARNING: Loop time exceeded, rescheduling immediately.
```

But PostgreSQL is never dropped from being a master (maybe this message is talking about the cluster state not the intention to demote PostgreSQL?)

```
-bash-4.2$ pg_controldata  | grep state
Database cluster state:               in production

```
```
[root@localhost ansible]# curl http://192.168.2.245:8008/
{"database_system_identifier": "6332181145039238210", "postmaster_start_time": "2016-09-20 08:56:54.475 AEST", "xlog": {"location": 67109408}, "patroni": {"scope": "postgresql", "version": "1.1"}, "state": "running", "role": "master", "server_version": 90504}
```

I would hope that this would happen to reduce the impact of writes being sent to the isolated master, then the other two nodes re-joining the network before the master did. 

In this case currently the master could be accepting writes for a long period, then when it re-joins the cluster these would be removed as it rewinds and syncs to the new master.

Is this intended?

Cheers,
James

Hi,

I found that downloading a file was slower than uploading (this is related to #28). I tried to find out why.

I wrote a simple script and ran it under the Python profiler. The script is:
```
import os
from getpass import getpass

from irods.session import iRODSSession
import irods.keywords as kw

pwd = getpass('iRODS password: ')

dest = 'siterg-imb'

with iRODSSession(host='...', port=1247, user='...', password=pwd, zone='...') as session:
    dom = session.data_objects
    options = {
        kw.FORCE_FLAG_KW: True,
    }

    dom.get('/.../stress-fs-1G', '/tmp/stress-fs-1G', **options)
```

Here is the profiler output:
```
         830461 function calls (829588 primitive calls) in 164.040 seconds

   Ordered by: cumulative time

   ncalls  tottime  percall  cumtime  percall filename:lineno(function)

issue_title: Authentication backend assumes username and password  (was marked as ToDo)
issue_body:
In __init__.py (line 228-229) the authentication backend assumes that a username and password is given in the check_auth function that is given in the api.method(...., authenticated=check_auth) function. 

    # TODO: this is an assumption
    X['arg_names'] = ['username', 'password'] + X['arg_names']

If you change this in: 

    auth_args = [x for x in authenticated.__code__.co_varnames]
    X['arg_names'] = auth_args + X['arg_names']

It would dynamically use the arguments that you define in the handler in ...method.(...authenticated=<handler>). In that way you could use:

    def check_auth(token):
        <do some magic token checking here for example>

Or any other means of authentication, instead of username and password.

Hello,
    I have completed a project using dynet 1.1. According to xor-mp and rnnlm-mp, I've implemented multi-processing and everything seems alright. No errors reported, train loss goes down after every epoch and I can use the model saved to predict. But I found that the model saved is always the initial model. So I debug my project and step into run_parent in mp.h, I found that every changes made by child's process doesn't seems to appear in run_parent. For example, I put an integer i in learner as its attribute and initialize it to 0. Then I change its value to 7 in LearnFromDatum, and print its value in SaveModel. Then I debug my project, step into run_parent. After runDataset, the child process us LearnFromDatum and print train loss. Then I use "print SaveModel()" in gdb, it shows that the attribute i in learner is still 0(it should have been changed in LearnFromDatum to 7 while training). So I get readlly confused, it seems that the learn in run_parent is a different object in run_child.
 Can you give me some advices to solve it? The project is not simple so it will be hard to recode it in dynet 2.0. Thanks!

issue_title: nbins is an integer in sigmet reader
issue_body:
Working off of master and reading in some IRIS data, I get the following traceback:

```
>>> rad = pyart.io.read('data/201211221820~~CONVOL:URP:WKR:RADAR:IRIS')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/broot/scratch/miniconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyart/io/auto_read.py", line 133, in read
    return read_sigmet(filename, **kwargs)
  File "/home/broot/scratch/miniconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyart/io/sigmet.py", line 136, in read_sigmet
    sigmet_data, sigmet_metadata = sigmetfile.read_data(full_xhdr=full_xhdr)
  File "pyart/io/_sigmetfile.pyx", line 180, in pyart.io._sigmetfile.SigmetFile.read_data (pyart/io/_sigmetfile.c:4596)
  File "pyart/io/_sigmetfile.pyx", line 298, in pyart.io._sigmetfile.SigmetFile._get_sweep (pyart/io/_sigmetfile.c:6285)
  File "pyart/io/_sigmetfile.pyx", line 752, in pyart.io._sigmetfile.convert_sigmet_data (pyart/io/_sigmetfile.c:10625)
TypeError: Cannot convert int to numpy.ndarray
```
Looking at line 752 in _sigmetfile.pyx, I see that it is passing in a view and an integer `nbins` to the function `_mask_gates_not_collected()`, which expects two ndarrays. The function this is called in already takes an argument `nbins`, and this one if-branch is the only place where `nbins` gets re-assigned (at line 677). I think we only need to change `nbins` to `nbin` at line 677 and 678, but I haven't been able to get conda-build to work properly.

issue_title: Missing system tests after pubsub redesign
issue_body:
The redesign effort (#3859) left behind only very minimal system tests.  The older implementation had much broader system test coverage:

- Listing topics and subscriptions in the client's project.
- Creating subscriptions with non-default settings.
- Listing subscriptions bound to a topic.
- Setting / getting IAM policy for topics and subscriptions
- Creating / seeking snapshots.

I noticed the missing tests today when I went looking for examples of updating a topic's IAM policy.

Whether due to oversight, or because the `buckets.update` / `objects.update` APIs weren't allowing it during implementation, the `update` methods (implemented in `_helpers._PropertyMixin`) don't pass their object's `user_project` (if set).

See #3474.

See also the `buckets.update` and `objects.update` method descriptions in the [discovery doc](https://www.googleapis.com/discovery/v1/apis/storage/v1/rest), which clearly show that `userProject` should be allowed.

issue_body:
What does it take to explicitly rollback a transaction?  For instance, I'm surprised that this doesn't work:

```python
>>> from google.cloud import datastore
>>> db = datastore.Client()
>>> e = datastore.Entity(db.key('Thingy'))

issue_title: Error when limit is not set for some queries
issue_body: When users run queries that have many results or run multiple queries concurrently, they can see an "internal error" (500).  When users set a limit on the query results, they may stop getting the error.  A work around for users is to set a limit for the number of results to return.  Then the user can use the end cursor from the first query's response as the start cursor for the next query request to get the next set of results.

Please consider tagging or releasing stable versions, this would make it much easier to keep track of updates.
Thank you.

issue_body:
* **Python 3.3**
```python

source activate pydill33 & python -c "import dill; g = (x for x in range(3)); print(g.__reduce_ex__(3)); next(g); pickled = dill.dumps(g); dill.loads(pickled)" & deactivate
```
produces

    obj = cls.__new__(cls, *args)
TypeError: object.__new__(generator) is not safe, use generator.__new__()
```

```python

issue_title: Target in subproject fails with relative dependency
issue_body:
# Problem
using the `--suproject_roots` option, it is possible to reference targets from a completely separate pants project found within the current project. All required dependencies are properly resolved when the targets within that subproject specify complete addresses, but fails when relative addresses are used to reference targets in the same build file (eg `dependencies=[':other_target']` ). In the former case, it appears to look for the relative target using the root of the subproject rather than the path to the BUILD file in which that the relative target is specified.

# Example
given the following structure
```
├── pants
├── pants.ini
└── src
    └── python
        └── my_project
            ├── BUILD
            └── my_sub_project
                └── src
                    └── python
                        └── BUILD
```
where
```python
# src/python/my_project/BUILD
python_library(
    dependencies=[
        'src/python/my_project/my_sub_project/src/python:sub_target_1',
    ],
)
```
and
```python
# src/python/my_project/my_sub_project/src/python/BUILD
python_library(
    name='sub_target_1',
    dependencies=[
        ':sub_target_2',
    ]
)
python_library(
    name='sub_target_2',
)
```
and
```ini
# pants.ini
[GLOBAL]
pants_version: 1.4.0rc1

subproject_roots = [
	'src/python/my_project/my_sub_project',
	]
```
The following command produces an unexpected error
 ```
./pants dependencies src/python/my_project
Exception caught: (<class 'pants.build_graph.address_lookup_error.AddressLookupError'>)

Exception message: Directory "Dir(path='src/python/my_project/my_sub_project')" does not contain build files.
```
# Workaround
Currently, if we update the subproject such that:
```python
# src/python/my_project/my_sub_project/src/python/BUILD
python_library(
    name='sub_target_1',
    dependencies=[
        'src/python:sub_target_2',
    ]
)
python_library(
    name='sub_target_2',
)
```
then the command works as expected
```
./pants dependencies src/python/my_project
src/python/my_project:my_project
src/python/my_project/my_sub_project/src/python:sub_target_1
src/python/my_project/my_sub_project/src/python:sub_target_2
```

issue_title: We have incorrect assumptions about how zinc finds javac
issue_body:
[Comments](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/2914fe05e064359a1381febc974982849937ba20/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jvm_compile/zinc/zinc_compile.py#L246) in `zinc_compile.py` imply that Zinc spawns an external javac process if it doesn't find `com.sun.tools.javac.Main` on its classpath. 

But from browsing the [Zinc source code](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/2914fe05e064359a1381febc974982849937ba20/src/scala/org/pantsbuild/zinc/Compiler.scala#L81), this seems inaccurate. In fact, Zinc spawns an external javac only if `-fork-java` or `-java-home` are set, and we set neither of them. So in fact if Zinc cannot find a javac Main class to run in-process, it'll throw a runtime error.

However things are even more complicated than this: Zinc doesn't actually try to classload `com.sun.tools.javac.Main` directly. Instead it delegates to [`javax.tools.ToolProvider.getSystemJavaCompiler`](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/javax/tools/ToolProvider.html#getSystemJavaCompiler--).

That method doesn't seem to care about `com.sun.tools.javac.Main` specifically. Instead it searches for the `com.sun.tools.javac.api.JavacTool` wrapper, and that later gets invoked through the `javax.tools.JavaCompiler` interface it implements.

So far, this only means that the comment is inaccurate.  But there's more going on: `getSystemJavaCompiler` searches for that `JavacTool` on the *bootclasspath* only, not the regular classpath.  And if it doesn't find it, it falls back to finding the compiler that comes with the current runtime (if that runtime is a JDK). 

We put tools.jar on the classpath to make zinc find it there instead of spawning one. But actually zinc will never spawn one, nor will it find it on the classpath. Instead it'll fall through to the one that comes with the JDK we're running Zinc on, which is what we wanted anyway!

So we can remove all that tools.jar complexity. Or, if we want to add functionality for using custom java compilers (and that's what I'm trying to do, and what prompted this discovery) then as far as I can tell we need to add it to the bootclasspath, not the regular classpath. 

My next step will be to try and concoct a test to expose this.

FAILURE: java com.pants.examples.hello.main.HelloMain ... exited non-zero (1)


               FAILURE
```

This appears to be semi-by design, so that we can highlight how bundles work. It might be nice to either point this out in the docs, or figure out a way to have our cake & eat it too.

TarballArtifact in artifact.py uses the sets the "dereference" option from the tarfile Python module (https://docs.python.org/2/library/tarfile.html#tarfile-objects) to True. See https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/cache/artifact.py#L99.

This option resolves symlinks, turning them into copies of the things they pointed to. In node_module targets, this means the symlinks under "<target dir>/node_modules/.bin" (such as "mocha -> ../mocha/bin/mocha") are resolved into copies of the executables files they point to. This breaks, for example, the common JS testing framework Mocha, which expects relative paths to be relative to its real bin directory (see https://github.com/mochajs/mocha/blob/master/bin/_mocha#L12).

For node_module targets, you can get around this with:
```
[cache.resolve.npm]
read: False
write: False
```

But talking to @patricklaw and @stuhood, it sounds like we can just drop the "'dereference': True" for all caching.

issue_title: Support CloudFormation intrinsic functions in SAM resources
issue_body:
We quite often use !Ref, !Sub, !Join in properties that are supposed to hold strings such as CodeUri, DefinitionUri, StageName (#19) etc. This is a perfectly valid CloudFormation, but the transformation logic that parses SAM does not resolve intrinsic functions before parsing the property as a string. So the transformation logic would fail and depending on the property it could either return a `validation error` or `internal failure`. Properties that are directly passed down to CloudFormation are unaffected by this problem.

This is a feature request to add support for resolving a subset of [CloudFormation intrinsics](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/intrinsic-function-reference.html) in the transformation logic. We care about only the subset of properties that the transform manipulates, like CodeUri. 

Following intrinsics will be supported:
- !Sub
- !Join

on following properties:

- CodeUri on AWS::Serverless::Function
- Role on AWS::Serverless::Function
- DefinitionUri on AWS::Serverless::Api
- StageName on  AWS::Serverless::Api

### Expected behaviour

The device tab and various features must be accessible.

### User-facing consequences

Admin won't be able to access device features like channel download etc.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Login as admin.
2. Try to open Device.

Kolibri version : kolibri 0.9.1
Operating system : ubuntu 14.04
Browser : Chrome

![q1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38091824/39130400-9c615326-472a-11e8-8656-2817b147d5fe.png)

At some time in the future, we can come back and redesign this if desired.

issue_title: plugins cannot be disabled when they don't exist
issue_body:
steps to reproduce:

1. create a new plugin (e.g. `hello`)
2. enable it: `kolibri plugin kolibri.plugins.hello enable`
3. delete the plugin. Now running kolibri throws an error: `ImportError: No module named hello`
4. try to disable the plugin: `kolibri plugin kolibri.plugins.hello disable` - thows an error: `kolibri.utils.cli.PluginDoesNotExist: Plugin does not exist`

At this point, `~/.kolibri/kolibri_settings.json` must be manually edited to remove the offending plugin

I'm not sure what the correct behavior is, but we probably don't want people to need to edit the settings file directly. Also, we may be able to log warnings with helpful information rather than raising exceptions.

The cards currently transition from a small drop-shadow to a large one on hover:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/26953519-a481ca34-4c5f-11e7-9d19-208abdd88e36.png) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/26953508-984260c6-4c5f-11e7-9c45-f35e0e87aba3.png)

issue_body:
### Observed behavior

In #3670, the query "add and subtract?" returns poor results, whereas "add and subtract" returns good results

### Expected behavior

the naive split logic (spaces only) should also split on other punctuation marks

This could look something like:

```python
words = [w for w in re.split('[?., ]', value) if w]
```

for here: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/3670/files#diff-7def35e64d458ec55c3b953c13824d05R115

### User-facing consequences

sub-optimal results. see https://docs.google.com/document/d/1zIGjlNyiXbJW4cABtakkqZkdjejGV-KRIc5XgwiDy2w/edit#



### Context

0.9

issue_title: Reinstate 'all' on recommendations page with more efficient pagination
issue_body:
## Summary

Currently we use page based pagination to show all content on the learn page. This can result in very slow API requests for large channels.

For now, this has been fixed by removing the `all` section from the learn page, but it should be reinstated for the anonymous user case, alongside a cursor based pagination that will allow for more efficient queries.

issue_title: DjangoCMS with Aldryn Newsblog urls clash - issue adding new non-blog pages
issue_body:
I am using Aldryn Newsblog and Aldryn FAQ in my DjangoCMS project. When trying to add a new page that is not a blog page, but just a normal CMS page, I get the following error: problem adding new page to Django CMS

![screen shot 2016-03-14 at 10 28 14](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2589523/13874964/e51155ec-ecf2-11e5-8e02-978edd109d68.png)

Seems like Aldryn Newsblog rewrites the urls and sets itself as the default root.

Here's my urls.py file:

```
from __future__ import absolute_import, print_function, unicode_literals

from cms.sitemaps import CMSSitemap
from django.conf import settings
from django.conf.urls import *  # NOQA
from django.conf.urls.i18n import i18n_patterns
from django.contrib import admin
from django.contrib.staticfiles.urls import staticfiles_urlpatterns

admin.autodiscover()

urlpatterns = patterns('',
    url(r'^admin/', include(admin.site.urls)),  # NOQA
    url(r'^sitemap\.xml$', 'django.contrib.sitemaps.views.sitemap',
        {'sitemaps': {'cmspages': CMSSitemap}}),
    url(r'^select2/', include('django_select2.urls')),
    url(r'^', include('cms.urls')),
)

# This is only needed when using runserver.
if settings.DEBUG:
    urlpatterns = patterns('',
        url(r'^media/(?P<path>.*)$', 'django.views.static.serve',  # NOQA
            {'document_root': settings.MEDIA_ROOT, 'show_indexes': True}),
        ) + staticfiles_urlpatterns() + urlpatterns  # NOQA
```
How to I tweak it to account for Aldryn Newsblog? 

Here is the corresponding StackOverflow question, earn +50 rep by answering: 
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/35986368/djangocms-with-aldryn-newsblog-urls-clash-issue-adding-new-non-blog-pages

issue_title: Use other methods to check if ipfs is installed
issue_body: Per #84, we use `ipfs --version` via Python subprocess to check if ipfs is installed before firing off subsequent ipwb procedures. @ibnesayeed mentioned that Py2's `find_executable` and Py3's `shutil.which` would incur much less expense to the system and it would be better to keep the call in Python instead of forking processes.

issue_title: Should we do separate repositories for the different APIs?
issue_body:
@e-lo asked:

Where does API / Examples / Gists live?

Preferably in separate repositories, tagged with OMX version #.  Currently the APIs of all code are in one place and move forward in commits together. I personally find this messy.   

What do you all think about having:
•	OMX.r
•	OMX.py
•	OMX.cpp
•	OMX.java
•	etc...
all as separate repositories?  There are [good and bad things](http://programmers.stackexchange.com/questions/161293/choosing-between-single-or-multiple-projects-in-a-git-repository) about this, but the main issue i see is one of the APIs moving forward w/out volunteers to move forward the other APIs and ended up with stale parts of a repo.  The major disadvantage I see is consolidated issue tracking/version control.

When creating a card registration you have to first create an instance on the server using the SDK, send the information down to the browser to use the card registration JS library and then back to the server to complete the registration. This is fine if you can keep the card registration object in memory, but if you're using a stateless backend it doesn't appear to be a way to retrieve the card registration object back with the SDK. 

Could I propose a solution that allows a user to store the id of the card registration in a database and then recall its ID and use it to recall a previous card registration like so?

```
recalled_card_registration = CardRegistration(id=recalled_id)
```

I used your example https://github.com/jwplayer/jwplatform-py/blob/master/examples/video_list_to_csv.py to query the video list and create a CSV file. It seems however, there are two bugs in this example:

issue_title: volumes_from doesn't mount if container also exports volumes
issue_body:
If I try to both export and import a volume, the volume doesn't get imported into the container.

Specifically, I'm trying to do something like this:

```
import docker

client = docker.Client(**docker.utils.kwargs_from_env(assert_hostname=False))
source = client.create_container(image="busybox:latest",
                                 command="tail -f /dev/null",
                                 name="source",
                                 volumes="/data",
                                 detach=True)
client.start(source['Id'])
dest = client.create_container(image="busybox:latest",
                               command="tail -f /dev/null",
                               name="dest",
                               volumes="/srv",
                               detach=True)
client.start(dest['Id'], volumes_from="source")

The imported `/data` volume is not present in the `dest` container:

issue_title: Provide True/False as variable inputs for constraints
issue_body:
**Application**
Sometimes I want to create a constraint for which I already know the values for some of the variables. I can create the constraint and then immediately fix the variable after, but it might be nice to do it on creation (also can memory/time).

**Proposed Solution**
I would like to be able to provide boolean values in the place of variables. This would be equivalent to fixing them.
```
csp = dwavebinarycsp.ConstraintSatisfactionProblem(dwavebinarycsp.BINARY)

def and_gate(in0, in1, out):
    return (in0 and in1) == out

csp.add_constraint(and_gate, ['a', 'b', False])
```

In this case the constraint would be the identical to

```
csp = dwavebinarycsp.ConstraintSatisfactionProblem(dwavebinarycsp.BINARY)

def nand(in0, in1):
    return not (in0 and in1)

csp.add_constraint(nand, ['a', 'b'])
```

issue_title: -o ttf-interpolatable removes necessary double points at start of curve
issue_body:
In [this font](https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/files/462704/Archive.zip), double points in the extended style are necessary to enable the font to interpolate correctly from narrow to wide. There's two issues that I'm seeing in the attached font. 

1) The starting point of the curve is being moved from the current placement—at the bottom of the curve. 
2) the double point present at the new starting point is being removed. It does appear that non-starting double points are preserved though.

For Noto, fontmake produces variable fonts that contain three axes (width, weight, custom). However, this “custom” axis is bogus and contains no useful data, so it should not be emitted in the output.

issue_title: We need a logo for the project

issue_title: processHandshake is unnecessarilly strict in UTF-8 decoding
issue_body:
We see cases where some browsers, in foreign locale's trigger the default strict decoding of utf-8 during processHandshake. It seems like lenient utf-8 decoding should be done.

The error:
```
encodings.utf_8 in decode
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xe9 in position 647: invalid continuation byte



Stacktrace (most recent call last):

  File "autopush/websocket.py", line 85, in wrapper
    return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "autopush/websocket.py", line 313, in processHandshake
    return self.parent_class.processHandshake(self)
  File "autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 2761, in processHandshake
    (self.http_status_line, self.http_headers, http_headers_cnt) = parseHttpHeader(self.http_request_data)
  File "autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 388, in parseHttpHeader
    raw = data.decode('utf8').splitlines()
  File "encodings/utf_8.py", line 16, in decode
    return codecs.utf_8_decode(input, errors, True)
```

Couldn't this use 'ignore' on the decode line to ignore anything it can't coerce?

issue_title: WebSocketClientFactory: unable to create new event loop
issue_body:
Hi all.

I am using Fedora 26 Workstation, with Python 3.6.2 and the asyncio backend of autobahn | python 17.9.3.

I have an issue where I am unable to create and use a new event loop after having closed another one using:

```
factory = WebSocketClientFactory("<URL>:443")
factory.protocol = MyClientProtocol
loop.run_until_complete(loop2.create_connection(factory, "<URL>", 443, ssl=True))
```

Here is a full code example, which demonstrates the issue:

```
import asyncio, json
from autobahn.asyncio.websocket import WebSocketClientProtocol, WebSocketClientFactory

class MyClientProtocol(WebSocketClientProtocol):

    def onConnect(self, response):
        print(response.peer)

    def onOpen(self):
        print("open")
        self.sendMessage(json.dumps({'command': 'subscribe', 'channel': "1010"}).encode("utf8"))

    def onMessage(self, payload, isBinary):
        print("message")
        print(json.loads(payload))

factory1 = WebSocketClientFactory("wss://api2.poloniex.com:443")
factory1.protocol = MyClientProtocol
loop1 = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop1.run_until_complete(loop1.create_connection(factory1, "api2.poloniex.com", 443, ssl=True))
try:
    loop1.run_forever()
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    pass

loop1.close()

asyncio.set_event_loop(asyncio.new_event_loop())

factory2 = WebSocketClientFactory("wss://api2.poloniex.com:443")
factory2.protocol = MyClientProtocol
loop2 = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop2.run_until_complete(loop2.create_connection(factory2, "api2.poloniex.com", 443, ssl=True))
try:
    loop2.run_forever()
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    pass

loop2.close()
```

```
import asyncio

async def hello_world():
    print("Hello World!")

coro1 = hello_world()
loop1 = asyncio.get_event_loop()

issue_title: Make training files universal, cut dependency on EFS
issue_body:
Presently the training files have hard coded paths on our EFS drive, rather than referring to files in S3.

https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/c9b52613cc3c5ab933bedddef6d63d06380e41b9/experiment_config/bidaf.json

We should use the same scheme we support on the command line, which downloads and caches files from http addresses.  This way, people external to AI2 can easily retrain our models.

issue_title: Archiving models at different points of training
issue_body: Currently the model is only archived at the end of the training, if I'm understanding correctly. When the model starts to overfit, I just quit the training, and then no archived model file is formed, and it's a hassle to evaluate / predict from the model. Basically one has to manually build model.tar.gz file to predict from it. Overall it would be nice to be able to predict from different timestamps without creating model.tar.gz for each.

issue_title: Output config.json in the same order to the input config file
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The config.json in the serialization directory looks quite different to the input config file. It is not easy to figure out which input config file was used for one experiment, especially when there are a lot of those experiments. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
While outputing config.json, can we use the same order of keys as the input config file? This can be accomplished by using an OrderedDict to load the original json.

This would remove the need for all of the special casing and reflection that I did to pass the metadata through correctly.  Basically we take all of the metadata-related code from [this PR](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/pull/216) and replace it with a `MetadataField`.  There will still be a _little_ bit of special casing, unless we also move the array creation code into a class method on the `Field` objects (probably on `Field` itself, overriden by `MetadataField`).  In particular, I mean this code: https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/fb73633a42053e8a0b2ce71448bad938f33a8442/allennlp/data/dataset.py#L150-L162

issue_title: Change functions that have filenames as arguments to use `Path` objects instead of strings
issue_body: To @joelgrus's point [here](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/pull/1232#issuecomment-392185359).  This will make it so we don't have to do `str(path)` everywhere.  One example of a place that needs to be changed is [`load_archive`](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/59a0e5a86b6294c2b0d6d9e1e32c10bccacfa883/allennlp/models/archival.py#L86); I'm sure there are more. (Nicely enough, if you remove the `str()` wrapper around the `str(path)` calls, it looks like [mypy will find the places for you.](https://gist.github.com/nelson-liu/f94a96265973a390066f45399718fe5e#file-allennlp-test-mypy-errors-txt-L10))

issue_title: How to connect to serve api via Docker
issue_body:
Looking at the api demo, I have tried to connect to the NER api in the same way the demo does from the docker host (where the port 8000 is correctly exported):

```
curl --header 'Accept:application/json' --header 'Content-Type:application/json' -v -X POST  -D '{"sentence": "Did Uriah honestly think he could beat The Legend of Zelda in under three hours?"}' http://localhost:8000/predict/named-entity-recognition
```

But I get no response from the api in the container, I have my container up and running and I have opened both the ports 8000 and 3000 in order to run the demo as well from the docker container:

```
$ docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 -p 3000: 3000 allennlp/allennlp:v0.4.1 python -m allennlp.run serve
6ffefe6e260c
$ docker ps
CONTAINER ID        IMAGE                      COMMAND             CREATED             STATUS              PORTS                                            NAMES
6ffefe6e260c        allennlp/allennlp:v0.4.0   "/bin/bash"         4 minutes ago       Up 4 minutes        0.0.0.0:3000->3000/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp   peaceful_wilson
```

In this way I can see the demo at http://localhost:3000, but from there I get the same api error on the port `8000`, i.e. a `failed` status of the `fetch` api in the demo.
I have checked the `flask` server that you are using, and runs on all interfaces (`0.0.0.0`), so this should be enough to make it working on the exposed port `8000` from the container's host.

By the way according to the docs, the docker run command `allennlp.run serve` should serve the api and the demo as well as described [here](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/commands/serve.py#L59), but for some reason it does not.

I've tried to download the model (decomposable-attention-elmo-2018.02.19.tar.gz) and run Textual Entailment prediction locally. There are two issues that I found:

1) The local version returns results that are different from the demo hosted @ demo.allennlp.org. In some cases, the difference is actually quite sizeable.
For example, with this JSONL file:

```
cat examples.jsonl                                                                                                                        
{"hypothesis": "He can influence and develop strong professional relationships", "premise": "John develops effective working relationships"}
```
and command
```
python -m allennlp.run predict decomposable-attention-elmo-2018.02.19.tar.gz examples.jsonl
```
produces the following result:

```
input:  {'hypothesis': 'He can influence and develop strong professional relationships', 'premise': 'John develops effective working relationships'}
prediction:  {"label_logits": [1.1014702320098877, -2.091261386871338, 2.0011751651763916], "label_probs": [0.2857194244861603, 0.011731510050594807, 0.7025490403175354]}
```

If you tried the demo, the results will be:
```
Entailment | 83.1%
Contradiction | 0.1%
Neutral | 16.8%
```
So Entailment returned by command line is 28.6% compared with 83.1% from the demo.

What version of code and model are being used @ demo.allennlp.org? Why does it produces such different results from command line version?

2) I measured performance of Textual Entailment model by loading the archive using ``load_archive`` and run ``Predictor.predict_json``. The execution time of ``predict_json`` method is around 200ms. The server has 28 cores and 110GB of RAM. Is this level of performance normal? I was expecting inference phase to be a lot faster.

Thanks.

issue_title: Examples in README are mixing tabs and spaces
issue_body:
For example, see https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-pipelines/blob/054db2faa0e768104ac43fd070cf3cc2e17ef69f/README.md#add_resource. The YAML example is:

```yaml
- run: add_resource
  parameters: 
    url: http://example.com/my-excel-file.xlsx
    sheet: 1
    headers: 2
- run: add_resource
  parameters:
    url: http://example.com/my-csv-file.csv
	encoding: "iso-8859-2"
```

Notice the red highlight before `encoding`. That's a TAB. I'm not sure which level the `encoding` should be in, so I couldn't send a PR, but there are a few examples like this in the README.

issue_title: Add possibility to connect more than 1 Crazyflie per program
issue_body:
Currently the Crazyflie lib is built on the assumption that one Python program connects to only one Crazyflie.

If we want to be able to fly swarm of Crazyflies using the lib, we need to be able to connect more than one Crazyflie.

One solution is to be able to create multiple Crazyflie object and to share Crazyradio.

issue_title: Fix alaska scraper to return approximate dates
issue_body:
Alaska has a frustrating website:

http://www.courtrecords.alaska.gov/webdocs/opinions/sp.htm

The problem is that rather than putting the items in the website with actual dates, they do quarterly date spans like:

> July - September 2016

These used to be the exception, I believe, but now they're the norm. So...we need to deal with them.

In https://github.com/freelawproject/courtlistener/issues/533 we're adding a new field to the DB for approximate dates. We should add support for this to Juriscraper as well. I think the process would be:

 - [ ] Update Juriscraper to have a new function, something like, `get_date_truthiness` (<-- That's probably bad). The new function would return True for every date that was known to be accurate, and would return False for ones that are estimates.

 - [ ] Update Alaska to return this value as appropriate. I suppose some of the time they used to provide accurate dates?

 - [ ] Update CL to have the needed field in the DB and to show it in appropriate places, maybe with an FAQ and a (?) thing.

 - [ ] Update the code that calls Juriscraper in CL so that it sets the values in the DB as we scrape.

issue_title: Remove Warnings from the user experience
issue_body:
I understand that printing all the UserWarnings and RuntimeWarnings is useful while developing on the source code of tsfresh, but as a user, it makes me wonder whether I screwed something up, or if there are bugs in the calculations themselves. 

I'm assuming it's all intentional, but that's an assumption I'd rather not have to make, and a not-ideal user experience. 

If you're ok with blocking warnings by default, I'm happy to implement that (I do something similar for [auto_ml](https://github.com/ClimbsRocks/auto_ml), since I assume it's my responsibility as the project's author to handle these warnings, and that my end users should not have to be bothered by my design choices).

issue_title: sendRawTransaction doesn't produce valid RPC request

issue_body:
* Version: 3.16.3
* Python: 3.5
* OS: linux


### What was wrong?

Processing events with `web3.utils.events.get_event_data` takes a rather long time, in the order of 1ms per event. This becomes quickly noticeable, when trying to decode event data for thousands of events. 

Please see my benchmarking script & output at https://gist.github.com/konradkonrad/87d76dd2526f39e6f5bad19b85374948

### How can it be fixed?

I don't want to rule out any user error here -- maybe there is a more efficient way to parse events data?

issue_title: Validate text and hexstr parameters are str type
issue_body:
* Version: 1

### What was wrong?

See [this comment](https://github.com/ethereum/eth-utils/pull/58#discussion_r167601284) about `bytes` being passed to `to_text` as `text=`. In that case `to_text()` returned a `bytes` value, which seems wrong.

### How can it be fixed?

Raise an exception if the `text` or `hexstr` parameters are not `str`, in any of the conversion methods.

issue_title: conda subcommands should reliably return with nonzero exit status on error
issue_body:
Problem:
```bash
$ conda build nonexistent  # silently passes with noop and exit status == 0
$
```

```bash
$ conda install /tmp/something.tar.bz2  # silently passes with noop and exit status == 0
$
```

Solution:
> Errors should never pass silently.

issue_title: `sys.path` contains --local installed python paths for a conda env
issue_body:
Two scipy core developers (Pauli and I) can reproduce this potential issue where creating a Python 3.6 conda environment results in a python executable in the environment that picks up python paths for `--local` installed packages.

His sample output:
```
$ conda create -n foo python=3.6
 ... 
$ source activate foo 
$ python 
Python 3.6.2 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Jul 20 2017, 13:51:32) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information. 
>> import sys 
>> sys.path 
['', '/home/pauli/.local/lib/conda/envs/foo/lib/python36.zip',
 '/home/pauli/.local/lib/conda/envs/foo/lib/python3.6',
 '/home/pauli/.local/lib/conda/envs/foo/lib/python3.6/lib-dynload',
 '/home/pauli/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages',
 '/home/pauli/.local/lib/conda/envs/foo/lib/python3.6/site-packages'] 
# 
# Note that /home/pauli/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages 
# appears in sys.path. This seems to only occur on Python 3.6. 
#
```

My sample output also contains stuff from a `--local` install
```
$ conda create -n foo python=3.6
...
$ source activate foo
$ python
Python 3.6.2 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Jul 20 2017, 13:51:32) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import sys
>>> sys.path
['', '/home/tyler/miniconda3/envs/foo/lib/python36.zip', '/home/tyler/miniconda3/envs/foo/lib/python3.6', '/home/tyler/miniconda3/envs/foo/lib/python3.6/lib-dynload', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/MDAnalysis-0.17.0.dev0-py3.6-linux-x86_64.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/joblib-0.11-py3.6.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mmtf_python-1.0.9-py3.6.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/GridDataFormats-0.3.3-py3.6.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/networkx-2.0-py3.6.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/biopython-1.70-py3.6-linux-x86_64.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/msgpack_python-0.4.8-py3.6-linux-x86_64.egg', '/home/tyler/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/decorator-4.1.2-py3.6.egg', '/home/tyler/miniconda3/envs/foo/lib/python3.6/site-packages']
```

I also confirmed that the issue seems to be Python 3.6-specific. Following the same procedure with Python 2.7 & 3.5 environments yielded sensible `sys.path` values that were isolated to the conda env.

The original discussion arose from our [development work](https://github.com/spacetelescope/asv/pull/539) on `asv`, where I noticed a path problem on Windows, but apparently this is reproducible on linux as well.

issue_title: Use repr() for roundtrip of mixed-quote string values
issue_body:
* Where an 'Value cannot be safely quoted' exception is thrown, return a repr() of the value instead.
* Rewrite affected unit tests

Spotted by @pierre-rouanet 

```python
import time

from pypot.creatures import PoppyErgoJr
from pypot.primitive.move import MoveRecorder, MovePlayer, Move


if __name__ == '__main__':
    jr = PoppyErgoJr(simulator='poppy-simu')

    mr = MoveRecorder(jr, 50, jr.motors)
    mr.start()
    time.sleep(1)
    mr.stop()

    with open('/tmp/bob.json', 'w') as f:
        mr.move.save(f)

    with open('/tmp/bob.json') as f:
        m = Move.load(f)

    # Fonctionne
    mp = MovePlayer(jr, mr.move)
    mp.start()
    mp.wait_to_stop()
    print('Everything ok')

    # Plante
    mp = MovePlayer(jr, m)
    mp.start()
    mp.wait_to_stop()
```

issue_title: GHDL : simulation options in wrong place on command line.
issue_body:
Using "set_sim_option" with ghdl's --elab-run command, the syntax expected by ghdl is that the elaboration options (e.g. "--std=08") are expected before the entity name, while the simulation runtime options (e.g. "--assert-level=none") must appear after it. 

However Vunit doesn't currently distinguish between elab and sim options, placing them both before the entity name on the command line.
Thus the following simulation options (elaboration options) work correctly, enabling code coverage via gcov (assuming ghdl built with the gcc back end)
```
vu.set_sim_option("ghdl.flags", ["-Wl,-lgcov"])
vu.set_sim_option("ghdl.flags", ["-Wl,--coverage"]) 
```
but the following simulation option (runtime option) does not:
`vu.set_sim_option("ghdl.flags", ["--assert-level=failure"])`
resulting in the message
"/usr/local/bin/ghdl: unknown option '--assert-level=failure' for command '--elab-run'

Command line usage of ghdl reveals:
```
ghdl --elab-run --std=08 --assert-level=none test
ghdl: unknown option '--assert-level=none' for command '--elab-run'

while
`ghdl --elab-run --std=08 test --assert-level=none`
works as expected.
```
I can work around this issue by changing the problematic asserts from severity Error (which abort the simulation) to severity Warning (which doesn't).

People somehow maintain packages working both with python 2 and python 3. In this case I think it will be extremely easy – looks like the only inconsistent thing is `except Exception, e` notation. Can I help you with this?

issue_title: Allow setting assert_hostname when using urllib3
issue_body:
In some instances, it may be necessary to set the `assert_hostname` when using the built-in `urllib3.HTTPSConnectionPool` in order to disable hostname verification (e.g., internal SSL certificates that do not supply a hostname).

It would be great if the parameter could be passed in without having to subclass. It may be worthwhile to also to support `assert_fingerprint` at the same time.

issue_title: [ACS] The name argument of the DC/OS browse command doesn't support auto-completion
issue_body:
If you type `az acs dcos browse -n <TAB>`, you see the file listings for the CWD, as opposed to the list of current ACS instance names within your subscription. The `-n` flag correctly supports this auto-completion for all other ACS commands, so this appears to be the only one that doesn't support it.

Additionally, auto-completion works for the `-g` parameter for the `az acs dcos browse` command, so it seems like it's just the `-n` flag that needs to be fixed. I'm running macOS Sierra, in case this issue may be platform-specific.

issue_title: [interactive shell] non-existent parameter "--vm-name" is required in managing VM with azure-cli interactive shell
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
 In managing VM with azure-cli interactive shell, non-existent parameter `--vm-name` is required
```
az>> vm start --name myvmname001 --resource-group myresourcegroup
az vm start: error: the following arguments are required: --vm-name
usage: az vm start [-h] [--verbose] [--debug]
                   [--output {json,jsonc,table,tsv}] [--query JMESPATH]
                   --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME --vm-name VM_NAME
                   [--no-wait]

az>> vm deallocate --name myvmname001 --resource-group myresourcegroup
az vm start: error: the following arguments are required: --vm-name
usage: az vm start [-h] [--verbose] [--debug]
                   [--output {json,jsonc,table,tsv}] [--query JMESPATH]
                   --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME --vm-name VM_NAME
                   [--no-wait]
```

**To Reproduce**
Entrying azure-cli intractive shell mode like this
```
$  az intractive
```
Then, executing the following command in an intractive shell
```
az>> vm start --name myvmname001 --resource-group myresourcegroup
```

**Expected behavior**
`--name` parameter should be required instead of `--vm-name`

**Environment summary**
- OS Version: `macOS 10.13.4 (17E202)` /  Kernel Version: `Darwin 17.5.0`
- CLI version: `azure-cli (2.0.33)`
- install methon: `pip`

**Additional context**
None

### Description
Microsoft.Network is releasing new feature called “virtual network private access” in the fist week of August. There're 2 Microsoft.Network API changes in this feature: 
1. New subnet.properties.privateAccessServices property
2. New subscription-wide API GET /privateAccessServices
We need your help to enable these in CLIv2. See details below. 

See CLIv1 info here: 
"https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/WAG/AzureNetworking/Engineering H  P/Network Resource Provider/Design/Service tunnel powershell changes.docx?web=1" 

Evgeny Agafonchikov (Akvelon) <v-evgena@microsoft.com> will send out swagger PR for these changes tomorrow

We discussed this over email with:
Mayuri Diwan <mayurid@microsoft.com>; 
Travis Prescott <trpresco@microsoft.com>; 
Jason Shaver <Jason.Shaver@microsoft.com>

1. PrivateAccessServices is a new property of a subnet: it’s an array of objects, each object has 1 property – “service” on input and 3 properties 

on output. 
It’s updated as part of PUT subnet or PUT virtualNetwork (subnet is a child of the virtual network)
INPUT (under subnet.properties in PUT subnet and PUT vnet requests)
         "privateAccessServices": [ 
              {
                    "service": “Microsoft.Storage”
                },
                { 
                    "service": “Microsoft.Sql”
                }
            ],
OUTPUT:
            "privateAccessServices": [ 
               { 
                    "provisioningState": “Updating|Deleting|Failed|Succeeded”,
                    "service": “Microsoft.Storage”,
                    "locations": ["eastus", "westus"]
                },
                { 
                    "provisioningState": “Updating|Deleting|Failed|Succeeded”,
                    "service": “Microsoft.Sql”,
                    "locations": ["eastus"]
                }
            ],
Minimum required api-version for the new property is 2016-12-01
PS spec Service tunnel powershell changes.docx?web=1
Feature spec: Storage account and SQL DB ACLs - NRP design.docx?web=1

2. We also need CLIv2 support for this subscription-wide GET API:
INPUT
GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Network/locations/{location}/privateAccessServices?api-version=2017-04-01
OUTPUT:
{
  "value": [
    {
      "id": "/subscriptions/s1/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices/Microsoft.Storage",
      "name": "Microsoft.Storage",
      "type": "Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices"
    },
    {
      "id": "/subscriptions/s1/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices/Microsoft.Sql",
      "name": "Microsoft.Sql",
      "type": "Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices"
    }
  ]
}
Minimum required api-version for this GET api is 2017-04-01
Note, that output is subscription and location specific. For example, subscriptions that have AllowServiceTunneling flag may see more services in the output and in some regions, there may be no services enabled for private access yet.

Note: Sample ARM template: \\scratch2\scratch\vladsero\vnetWithST.json 
You’ll need a prod subscription with Microsoft.Network/AllowServiceTunneling feature flag to try this (“service tunneling” is old/internal name of the feature). Anitha Adusumilli <Anitha.Adusumilli@microsoft.com> can set it for you.

issue_title: secret-identifier does not support mixed case key vault name
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Using the secret-identifier switch with a mixed-case key vault fails to work:
az sf cluster create --secret-identifier https://MyPrivateKeyVault.vault.azure.net/secrets/mycertname/mycertid

However, if you create your key vault with a lowercase name, the following does work:
az sf cluster create --secret-identifier https://myprivatekeyvault.vault.azure.net/secrets/mycertname/mycertid

The problem occurs

**To Reproduce**
Try to create a cluster using an existing mixed-case key vault.

**Expected behavior**
Mixed-case key vault names should be supported since you can easily create mixed case key vaults within the Azure web interface. Additionally, the error shown the user is currently cryptic and unclear unless pull up the python code and review it.

**Additional context**
This problem can be corrected by adding `.lower()` to the vault name comparison in custom.py:1333 so that the new code reads:
`vault = [v for v in vaults if v.name.lower() == vault_name]`

instead of:
`vault = [v for v in vaults if v.name == vault_name]`

### Description

The description for `az role assignment list` should give details on the default values provided.  This command is confusing in use right now as you can create a role assignment against a resource group, but not see it in the assignment list (without a --all), but there are no details on what is or is not shown.  

Current:
```
    az role assignment list: List role assignments.

Arguments
    --all              : Show all assignments under the current subscription.
```

I propose something like:

```
    az role assignment list: List role assignments.  By default, only assignments scoped to subscription will be displayed.  To view assignments scoped by resource or group, use --all. 

Arguments
    --all              : Show all assignments under the current subscription.
```

issue_title: query operator with JMESPath doesn't handle a 'dash' (-) properly
issue_body:
If a tag on a resource has a name that includes a `-` the query fails incorrectly.

**To Reproduce**
1. Assign a tag of `CREATED-BY` to a resource group in Azure with a value of 'foobar'
2. Run the Query
`az group list -o tsv --query "[?tags.CREATED-BY == 'foobar']"`

**Expected behavior**
Normally the results will show the item.

**Actual result**
The error shows as 
```
az group list: error: argument --query: invalid jmespath_type value: "[?tags.CREATED-BY == 'foobar']"
```
If you run a similar query on http://jmespath.org/  as below, it works fine
```
locations[?state == 'WA' && foo-bar == 'foobar'].name | sort(@)
```

Using data of:
```json
{
  "locations": [
    {"name": "Seattssle", "state": "WA", "foo-bar": "foobar"},
    {"name": "New York", "state": "NY"},
    {"name": "Bellevue", "state": "WA"},
    {"name": "Olympia", "state": "WA"}
  ]
}
```

**Environment summary**
This is verified to fail in the Azure Cloud Shell. 

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

The Knack-based core made changes to certain methods that are utilized by the azure.common package, resulting in breaking changes. These changes were needed because the Knack-based core needs a CLI context to work.

- [ ] _profile.py Profile constructor
- [ ] get_active_cloud method

The fix for these cases will be to make cli_ctx an optional parameter which, if not supplied, will be created for the duration of the call.

cc/ @annatisch @lmazuel @mayurid

issue_title: acr says "Please verify if docker daemon is running properly." despite docker daemon running
issue_body:
**Problem**
After performing a login to the container registry: `az acr login --name <acrName>`
acr says "Please verify if docker daemon is running properly." despite docker daemon running.

**Suspected cause**
The root cause of this issue seems to be that [acr/custom.py](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/command_modules/azure-cli-acr/azure/cli/command_modules/acr/custom.py) tries to run `docker ps` which needs elevated rights on some systems. So the command returns a non-zero error and throws an exception.

So a solution for the cli might be to provide a better warning.

A **workaround/solution** for people encountering this issue is to run the command with `sudo`:
`sudo az acr login --name <acrName>`

---

### Environment summary

Install Method: apt-get
azure-cli (2.0.22)
acr (2.0.16)
